events.change_summary: Intent check: comprehensive trader toolkit expansion initiative

events.summary: Intent check written: identifies this as a multi-feature enhancement initiative requiring prioritization. Asks builder to clarify which features should land in Phase 1 vs future phases, and whether to expand the TUI or create a new UI tier.

events.conflict_message: sync lock timeout: another sync is in progress (waited 10s) at /Users/nickmoore/setups/.defract/.sync.lock

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2): Phase 1 entry/exit levels + Phase 2 earnings calendar.

events.change_summary: Full scope: focused Phase 1 (entry/exit/stop levels + R:R) and Phase 2 (earnings calendar), with the broader AI/heavy-data vision deferred to backlog. Detailed phases, architecture and design stages skipped.

events.rationale: New user-visible functionality (entry/exit levels + earnings warnings) but tightly contained. Skipping design: all output extends existing TUI detail panel, plain table, and symbol-command patterns with no new presentation surface or visual-design decisions. Skipping architecture: changes are additive within established subsystems (data/analysis/models/ui) reusing existing config-threading, pure-function, and rate-limited-fetch patterns — no new system boundaries. Two phases warrant review (heuristic level placement; an added per-setup API call), so review and release stages are retained.

events.decision: Scope the broad 11-feature brief down to a shippable Phase 1 (entry/exit/stop levels + R:R) and Phase 2 (earnings calendar); defer the 9 AI/heavy-data feature areas to the backlog.
events.rationale: The brief is far too large for one task. Entry/exit (the builder's lead ask) and earnings (explicitly requested) build directly on existing data and patterns, deliver immediate trader value, and require no new AI integration or historical-data feeds. Shipping these first validates direction before investing in the heavier subsystems.

events.decision: Compute entry/stop/target from a configurable percentage-and-gap model (--stop-pct, --reward-mult) instead of ATR-based volatility stops.
events.rationale: The pipeline does not fetch intraday/historical OHLC candles, and Finnhub's free tier restricts candle data. The existing atr() function cannot be fed real inputs without new, rate-limited (and possibly unavailable) calls. A percentage model uses only data already fetched (prev_close, premarket_price), keeps Phase 1 dependency-free and unit-testable, and bounds stop distance sensibly on large gaps. ATR refinement is noted as future work.

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2. Narrowed the 11-feature brief to a shippable two-phase task: Phase 1 adds direction-aware entry/stop/target levels with a risk/reward ratio, computed from existing quote data plus configurable --stop-pct/--reward-mult flags (no new data dependency); Phase 2 adds an earnings-calendar fetch flagging imminent earnings. Both surface across the TUI detail panel, plain table, and symbol command. ATR-based stops deferred (no candle data on free tier). The other 9 brief feature areas (correlations, AI news synthesis, conversational analyst, sentiment, anomaly detection, scenario modeling, charting, backtesting, order flow) are deferred and proposed as backlog items. Workflow: full-feature/standard, stages scope → implementation → review → release (design and architecture skipped — additive changes within existing subsystems and UI patterns). 12 requirements, 9 acceptance criteria, 2 phases.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Implementing Phase 1: actionable entry/stop/target levels and R:R

events.description: Starting phase 1: Actionable entry and exit levels

events.details: cargo test: 93 passed (88 baseline + 5 new trade_levels tests covering LONG, SHORT, R:R-equals-reward-mult, wider-knobs, and zero-price boundary). cargo build clean with no new warnings. CLI flags --stop-pct and --reward-mult parse correctly.

events.decision: Implementation: trade_levels takes an is_long: bool rather than the direction &str, and Setup carries an owned levels: TradeLevels field (not Option).
events.rationale: An is_long bool keeps the pure function self-contained and trivially unit-testable without coupling to Setup::direction()'s string format; the screener derives is_long from ticker.gap_pct() >= 0.0, matching direction() exactly. Every scanned setup always gets levels, so an owned field is simpler than an Option that would never be None.

events.decision: Implementation: replaced the file-level #![allow(dead_code)] in indicators.rs with per-function #[allow(dead_code)] on the still-unused functions (vwap, relative_volume, gap_pct, atr, ema).
events.rationale: The phase asked to remove trade_levels from the dead-code allowance now that it is used. A blanket file-level allow would have kept trade_levels suppressed too; per-function allows let the compiler genuinely verify trade_levels is used while keeping the unrelated pure functions warning-free, satisfying the no-new-warnings acceptance criterion.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Actionable entry and exit levels

events.summary: Added direction-aware trade levels (entry/stop/target/risk-reward) to every setup. New pure trade_levels() in indicators.rs with 5 unit tests; TradeLevels struct + owned field on Setup; --stop-pct (5.0) and --reward-mult (2.0) flags threaded through ScreenerConfig; levels rendered in the plain table, the Symbol subcommand, and the TUI detail panel. Build clean, 93 tests pass.

items.description: Run `cargo run -- --plain` and confirm each setup row shows `ENTRY`, `STOP`, `TARGET`, and `R:R` values alongside the existing columns.
On a gap-up (LONG) row, verify `STOP` is below `ENTRY` and `TARGET` is above it; on a gap-down (SHORT) row, verify `STOP` is above `ENTRY` and `TARGET` is below it.
Run `cargo run -- --stop-pct 10 --reward-mult 3` and compare the same symbol against the defaults — stops should be visibly wider and targets proportionally further, with `R:R` reading `3.00`.
Run `cargo run -- symbol NVDA` and confirm the output prints `Direction`, `Entry`, `Stop`, `Target`, and `Risk/Reward` lines.
Launch the TUI with `cargo run`, select a setup, and confirm the detail panel shows a `Trade Plan` line with entry, a red `Stop`, a green `Target`, and `R:R`.
Confirm `cargo test` reports 93 passing and `cargo build` finishes with no warnings.

events.note: Implementing Phase 2: earnings calendar fetch and imminent-earnings warnings

events.details: cargo test: 99 passed (93 baseline + 6 new). New tests cover earnings_is_imminent within-window, on-boundary, today, beyond-window, past-date, and None cases. cargo build clean with no new warnings in touched modules.

alternatives_considered: Store next_earnings as Option<String> (avoids NaiveDate but loses type-safe comparison)
Fetch earnings during candidate enrichment alongside the catalyst (would exceed the top_n call budget)
events.decision: Implementation: store next_earnings as Option<chrono::NaiveDate> on Setup and fetch it once per scored setup after sorting and truncating to top_n, not during candidate enrichment.
events.rationale: Fetching after truncation guarantees the extra rate-limited call runs at most top_n times (R12), instead of up to top_n*2 candidate times. NaiveDate (chrono already has the serde feature) keeps the imminence comparison type-safe and serializable, matching the existing Serialize/Deserialize convention on models. earnings_is_imminent is a free-standing pure function so the window logic is unit-testable without the wall clock, while Setup::earnings_imminent wraps it for call sites that have a Setup.

events.summary: Added earnings awareness to every scored setup. New get_next_earnings() on FinnhubClient hits /calendar/earnings over a 90-day horizon, reusing get<T>() and the 1.1s sleep, degrading gracefully to None. Setup gains an Option<NaiveDate> next_earnings field, an earnings_imminent() method, and a pure earnings_is_imminent() predicate (default 5-day window) with 6 unit tests. The screener fetches earnings once per scored setup after truncation (≤ top_n). Earnings date + imminent flag render in the plain table, the symbol subcommand, and the TUI detail panel. Build clean, 99 tests pass.

items.description: Run `cargo run -- --plain` and confirm each setup row now shows an `EARNINGS` column displaying a date (e.g. `2026-07-30`), or `—` when no upcoming date is known.
On a row whose company reports within 5 days, confirm the earnings cell shows the date followed by `SOON`; on rows further out, confirm only the bare date appears.
Run `cargo run -- symbol NVDA` and confirm an `Earnings:` line prints a date (with an `(imminent)` marker when within 5 days) or `—` when none is upcoming.
Launch the TUI with `cargo run`, select a setup, and confirm the detail panel's `Earnings:` line shows the date, rendered bold-yellow with `(imminent)` when within the window and `—` when unknown.
Confirm a symbol with no upcoming earnings (or when the `/calendar/earnings` call fails) renders `—` without error and the scan still completes.
Confirm `cargo test` reports `99 passed` and `cargo build` finishes with no warnings.

events.summary: All phases approved — implementation ready for review.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.details: cargo test: 99 passed (88 baseline + 5 trade_levels tests + 6 earnings_is_imminent tests), 0 failed. cargo build clean with no warnings.

events.change_summary: APPROVE: All 9 acceptance criteria pass with concrete code evidence. Both phases are complete: direction-aware trade levels render across all three output surfaces, and the earnings calendar integration degrades gracefully and bounds extra API calls to top_n. Build is clean with no warnings and 99 tests pass.

events.rationale: 9/9 acceptance criteria passed, 2/2 automated checks passed. All trade levels render correctly across TUI detail, plain table, and symbol subcommand. Earnings calendar integration degrades gracefully, bounds API calls to top_n, and surfaces imminent flag and neutral placeholder as required. No security issues or convention violations found.

events.summary: APPROVE — all 9 acceptance criteria pass, both automated checks pass (99 tests, clean build). Trade levels and earnings calendar are fully implemented and wired across all three output surfaces.

workflow.rationale: New user-visible functionality (entry/exit levels + earnings warnings) but tightly contained. Skipping design: all output extends existing TUI detail panel, plain table, and symbol-command patterns with no new presentation surface or visual-design decisions. Skipping architecture: changes are additive within established subsystems (data/analysis/models/ui) reusing existing config-threading, pure-function, and rate-limited-fetch patterns — no new system boundaries. Two phases warrant review (heuristic level placement; an added per-setup API call), so review and release stages are retained.

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 2: Earnings awareness — complete

Every scored setup now carries its next upcoming earnings date and an imminent-earnings flag, surfaced across the plain table, the `symbol` subcommand, and the TUI detail panel. The fetch degrades gracefully and runs at most once per scored setup.

### What was built

- **`src/data/finnhub.rs`** — added `get_next_earnings(symbol) -> Option<NaiveDate>` hitting `/calendar/earnings?from=&to=&symbol=` over a 90-day horizon, reusing the shared `get<T>()` helper and the 1.1s rate-limit sleep. It degrades like `get_top_catalyst`: a failed or empty response resolves to `unwrap_or_default()` and the method returns the earliest non-past date (or `None`), never aborting the scan. Added `EarningsCalendarResp` (with `#[derive(Default)]`) and `EarningsEntry` deserialization structs.
- **`src/models/setup.rs`** — added `next_earnings: Option<NaiveDate>` to `Setup`; a `Setup::earnings_imminent(window_days)` method; a free-standing pure `earnings_is_imminent(earnings, today, window_days)` predicate (past dates never count, today and the window boundary do); and `EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS` (default 5). Six unit tests cover within-window, on-boundary, today, beyond-window, past-date, and the None case.
- **`src/models/mod.rs`** — re-export `earnings_is_imminent` and `EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS`.
- **`src/analysis/screener.rs`** — sets `next_earnings: None` at build time, then after sorting and truncating to `top_n`, fetches the earnings date once per surviving setup. This bounds the extra calls to ≤ `top_n` (R12) rather than once per candidate.
- **`src/main.rs`** — `print_table` gains an `EARNINGS` column (date, or "—", with a `SOON` suffix when imminent); the `Symbol` subcommand fetches and prints an `Earnings:` line with an `(imminent)` marker.
- **`src/ui/dashboard.rs`** — `render_detail` gains an `Earnings` line showing the date (or "—"), bold-yellow with an `(imminent)` marker when within the window. Fits within the existing 10-line detail block.

### Deviations from plan

- None of substance. The imminence threshold lives in a shared `EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS` constant rather than a CLI flag, since R9 specifies a fixed default window and no flag was requested.

### Verification

- `cargo build` clean, no new warnings.
- `cargo test`: 99 passed (93 baseline + 6 new earnings tests).
- Plain table shows an `EARNINGS` column; `symbol` and TUI detail show the earnings line; missing dates render "—".

### Files changed

`src/data/finnhub.rs`, `src/models/setup.rs`, `src/models/mod.rs`, `src/analysis/screener.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `src/ui/dashboard.rs`.
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 7 files changed across 2 phases

All 9 acceptance criteria pass with concrete code evidence. Both phases are complete: direction-aware trade levels render across all three output surfaces, and the earnings calendar integration degrades gracefully and bounds extra API calls to top_n. Build is clean with no warnings and 99 tests pass.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| cargo test | PASS | 99 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped |
| cargo build | PASS | Finished dev profile with no warnings in touched modules |

### Acceptance Criteria (9/9 passed)

- [x] AC-1: Running `cargo run -- --plain` shows an Entry, Stop, Target, and R:R value for each setup row; verified by inspecting the printed table. — PASS: main.rs:201 header includes ENTRY, STOP, TARGET, R:R columns; main.rs:228-237 row format prints s.levels.entry, s.levels.stop, s.levels.target, s.levels.risk_reward
- [x] AC-2: For a gap-up (LONG) setup, the stop is below the entry and the target is above it; for a gap-down (SHORT) setup, the stop is above the entry and the target is below it; verified by a unit test over both directions. — PASS: indicators.rs:224-233 trade_levels_long_stop_below_target_above asserts stop < entry and target > entry; indicators.rs:236-245 trade_levels_short_stop_above_target_below asserts stop > entry and target < entry
- [x] AC-3: `cargo run -- --stop-pct 10 --reward-mult 3` produces wider stops and proportionally further targets than the defaults; verified by comparing output for the same symbol. — PASS: main.rs:43-48 defines --stop-pct and --reward-mult flags; main.rs:84-85 threads them into ScreenerConfig; indicators.rs:254-259 unit test trade_levels_wider_stop_pushes_target_further verifies this property
- [x] AC-4: The risk/reward ratio equals the reward multiple within rounding for the default model; verified by a unit test. — PASS: indicators.rs:247-251 trade_levels_risk_reward_equals_reward_mult asserts (levels.risk_reward - 3.0).abs() < EPS with entry=42, stop_pct=10, reward_mult=3
- [x] AC-5: The level-computation function has unit tests covering LONG, SHORT, and a boundary (e.g. zero/near-zero gap) case; verified by `cargo test`. — PASS: indicators.rs:222-270 contains five tests: LONG, SHORT, R:R-equals-reward-mult, wider-knobs, and zero-price boundary; cargo test shows 99 passed
- [x] AC-6: The TUI detail panel and the `cargo run -- symbol NVDA` output both display the entry, stop, target, and R:R; verified by running each. — PASS: dashboard.rs:218-235 render_detail shows Trade Plan, Entry, Stop (red), Target (green), R:R; main.rs:125-129 symbol subcommand prints Direction, Entry, Stop, Target, Risk/Reward
- [x] AC-7: When a setup's company has earnings within the near-term window, an imminent-earnings indicator appears in the plain table and TUI detail; verified by running a scan during a known earnings week or with a stubbed date. — PASS: main.rs:210-219 appends SOON suffix when s.earnings_imminent(EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS) is true; dashboard.rs:241-246 renders date with (imminent) in bold-yellow; setup.rs:219-225 six unit tests verify the predicate logic
- [x] AC-8: A setup with no upcoming earnings date renders without error and shows a neutral placeholder (e.g. "—"); verified by running a scan. — PASS: main.rs:218 None arm returns "—".to_string(); main.rs:141 symbol subcommand returns "—"; dashboard.rs:250 renders Span::raw("—") for None; setup.rs:254-257 unit test earnings_imminent_none_is_false
- [x] AC-9: `cargo build` and `cargo test` succeed with no new warnings introduced for the touched modules; verified by running both. — PASS: cargo build output: Finished dev profile with no warnings; cargo test: 99 passed, 0 failed

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- trade_levels takes is_long: bool rather than the direction &str; Setup carries owned TradeLevels (not Option) since every scanned setup always gets levels computed
- Replaced file-level #![allow(dead_code)] with per-function #[allow(dead_code)] on the still-unused functions so trade_levels is genuinely verified as used
- next_earnings stored as Option<chrono::NaiveDate>; fetched once per scored setup after truncation to top_n (not during candidate enrichment) to bound extra calls to <= top_n (R12)
- Imminence threshold is a shared EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS constant, not a CLI flag, since R9 specified a fixed default and no flag was requested

## Required Changes

None.

decisions: trade_levels takes is_long: bool rather than the direction &str; Setup carries owned TradeLevels (not Option) since every scanned setup always gets levels computed

changeAnnotations.note: Adds the pure trade_levels() function with 5 unit tests; removes the blanket dead-code suppression in favour of per-function attributes so the new function is genuinely compiler-verified as used
Adds TradeLevels struct, owned levels field on Setup, optional next_earnings date field, earnings_imminent() method, and the free-standing earnings_is_imminent() predicate with 6 unit tests
Re-exports TradeLevels, earnings_is_imminent, and EARNINGS_IMMINENT_WINDOW_DAYS so call sites in main.rs and dashboard.rs can access them without qualifying the full path
Extends ScreenerConfig with stop_pct and reward_mult knobs; computes trade levels per setup during scan(); fetches next earnings date once per surviving setup after truncation to top_n
Adds --stop-pct and --reward-mult CLI flags and threads them into ScreenerConfig; print_table gains ENTRY, STOP, TARGET, R:R, and EARNINGS columns; Symbol subcommand prints the five new fields
render_detail gains a Trade Plan line showing entry/stop/target/R:R and an Earnings line with imminent marker, fitting within the existing 10-line detail block
Adds get_next_earnings() hitting /calendar/earnings over a 90-day horizon, reusing the shared get<T>() helper and 1.1s rate-limit sleep, degrading gracefully to None on failure
